source_jobs.title: QA Automation & DevOps Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

We are seeking a highly technical and detail-oriented QA Automation & DevOps Specialist to improve Double the Donation’s product velocity and deployment reliability. This role will sit at the intersection of Product and Engineering, owning the transition from manual testing workflows to an automated, scalable QA and deployment pipeline.

This individual will be responsible for building and maintaining automated test systems, reducing bottlenecks in the development lifecycle, and supporting infrastructure reliability. The goal is to enable faster, higher-quality releases while allowing Product Designers and Engineers to focus on high-value work.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, implement, and maintain automated testing frameworks (e.g., Playwright) to ensure coverage of critical product workflows

 - Translate product requirements and user flows into scalable, automated test cases

 - Identify gaps in current QA processes and proactively improve testing coverage and reliability

 - Collaborate closely with Product Designers and Engineers to ensure features meet acceptance criteria and quality standards

 - Own and optimize the testing lifecycle within the development process, reducing manual QA dependency

 - Support CI/CD pipeline management and improvements to ensure smooth and reliable deployments

 - Monitor application performance and deployment stability, identifying and resolving issues proactively

 - Document testing frameworks, processes, and best practices for internal teams

 - Track and report on testing coverage, bug trends, and deployment quality metrics
   


SETUP

 - This is a work-from-home setup

 - Target of 42.5 billable hours per week

This role follows a consistent daily rhythm that aligns with US Eastern business hours while giving you time for rest, personal tasks, and balance throughout your day.

Here’s what a typical day looks like:

 - Start your day with a 4.5-hour work block: 9:30am–2:00pm (PHT)

 - Enjoy your afternoon free: 2:00pm–8:00pm (PHT)

 - Finish your workday with another 4.5-hour block: 8:00pm–12:30am (PHT)

 - Sleep/rest: 12:30am–9:30am (PHT)

This structure offers a long midday break while supporting strong collaboration with US-based teams during their business hours.

 - Work-from-home setup
   
   
   [https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/4802dda9-c742-4abd-893c-f4d51c51e50e/44d12927-ed39-4f53-b518-b616cab2c1cc/Split.png]

Note: All of these times would shift 1 hour later when the time difference switches to 13 hour differences from 12 hour differences



REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in the Philippines with consistent availability for a long-term role

 - Exceptional English verbal and written communication skills

 - Strong academic background with an outstanding General Weighted Average

 - Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related technical field

 - Proven experience in a SaaS or fast-paced software development environment

 - Strong proficiency in automated testing frameworks (preferably Playwright)

 - Experience working with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps tools (e.g., GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or equivalent

source_jobs.title: Sales Systems Engineer (AI Enablement & Sales Operations Focus)
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

We are seeking a highly technical, systems-oriented Sales Systems Engineer to spearhead the modernization of Double the Donation’s sales operations. In this role, you won't just support the status quo—you will architect, build, and deploy the technical infrastructure that scales our sales team.

The ideal candidate is a corporate-experienced professional who possesses deeper technical skills than a traditional sales team. You are someone who thinks in data structures, API integrations, and AI workflows. You don't just follow a process; you automate it out of existence.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - AI Enablement & Agent Deployment: Own the deployment and continuous refinement of HubSpot Prospecting Agents and custom in-house AI tools (e.g., custom Gemini Gems, Sales Coach bots). Build, train, and maintain AI-driven digital sales training guides and internal resources.

 - Advanced Data Enrichment & Scraping: Solve complex data enrichment challenges. Architect automated data pipelines to scrape conference apps, web-based membership lists, and XML data from ProPublica, seamlessly auto-updating HubSpot or central databases.

 - Outbound Automation Infrastructure: Own the setup, configuration, and execution of automated outreach tools (e.g., RB2B, Instantly, or custom in-house equivalents). Leverage tools like BuiltWith, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and Clay to build, clean, and enrich highly targeted prospect lists.

 - Integration Partner Workflows: Design and execute data-scraping workflows for new integration partners—identifying platform users, injecting them into the CRM as clean records, and appending them with Nonprofit Software data to trigger automated outreach.

 - CRM Engineering & Architecture: Serve as the technical administrator for HubSpot. Optimize lead routing logic, build custom lead-scanner applications for conferences, manage complex data hygiene, and eliminate operational bottlenecks.

 - Reporting & Systems Documentation: Build advanced dashboards tracking key performance metrics (conversion rates, response times, pipeline health) and write flawless documentation/SOPs for the systems you build.
   
   


SETUP

 - This is a work-from-home setup.

 - You would work during US EST Hours (Approx. 8 or 9 am to 5 or 6 pm EST)

 


REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in the Philippines with consistent availability for a long-term role

 - Experience: 3–6+ years of experience in Sales Operations, RevOps, or Systems Engineering in a B2B SaaS or corporate environment. (This is not an entry-level position.)

 - Technical Stack Mastery: Advanced proficiency with HubSpot, Make.com http://Make.com, Clay, Zapier, or similar middleware automation platforms.

 - Data & Scraping Skills: Proven track record of handling "hard builds"—including XML/JSON data scraping, web scraping, and manipulating API webhooks to connect disparate systems.

 - AI/LLM Aptitude: Direct experience building and deploying AI agents, customizing LLMs for business logic, or engineering prompts for practical sales applications.

 - Mindset: A consultative approach. You can take a loose operational problem, turn it into a technical blueprint, and deliver a po

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer / Software Developer
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

We are looking to hire individuals based in the Philippines to help us better serve our clients from the nonprofit and mission-driven space.

As a Tech Team Developer, you will be:

 - Implementing features in our core application

 - Researching bug requests and quickly resolving issues

 - Participating in our bi-weekly sprint planning

 - Handling advanced troubleshooting on the tech support side when our Client Success and Support teams need assistance for clients

With 100 Filipino team members already thriving in our company, we are excited to expand this growing team with new talent!

If you’re in the Philippines, have a knack software development, and are eager to drive growth for nonprofits, we'd love to hear from you. Join us and be a part of a team where your contributions have a lasting impact.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Provide technical support on the side

 - Help clean, maintain, and document clojure code

 - Collaborate with our tech team identify, define, and implement software features

 - Participate in code reviews and contribute to improving code quality

 - Troubleshoot and debug complex technical issues

 - Help build and maintain a robust testing infrastructure

 - Contribute to the overall technical direction of the product

 - Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Google Cloud Platform, etc.)

 - Experience with DevOps practices and tools (CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure automation)

 - Experience with data analysis libraries

 - DevOps Support

 - Deployment to Production




OUR CODEBASE

Our core application is built in Clojure. Experience with Clojure or a Functional Programming Language is a nice-to-have, but not necessary to start.

 - There are some features / parts of our application where individuals can contribute on Day 1 without Clojure knowledge.

 - We expect team members to grow their Clojure skills rapidly, and part of your long-term compensation is based on becoming a strong contributing team member across our full codebase.
   


ADDITIONAL TECH STACK DETAILS

 - Our systems run on Microsoft Azure Cloud computing platform.

 - Postgres is our primary data store.

 - Elasticsearch powers our search experience.

 - We utilize Azure Blob storage for file uploads and miscellaneous object storage.

 - We leverage Github Actions for CI/CD process automation.

 - We use Capistrano for deployment orchestration.

 - We rely on Sendgrid for email delivery.

 - Our app is a Single-page web application (SPA) built atop React.

 - Bootstrap for CSS for styling

 - We use Playwright for end-to-end testing and browser automation.

 - Datadog for monitoring and observability

 - Zoho (crucially: not Jira!) for issue tracking
   
   


WHAT MAKES A STRONG CANDIDATE

 - Based in the Philippines with consistent availability for a long-term role.

 - Consistent availability and looking for a long-term position

 - Highly organized and outstanding organizational ability

 - Exceptional English verbal and written communication skills

 - Strong academic background from someone who graduated with an outstanding General Weighted Average (often honors graduates) from one of the Big 4 universiti

source_jobs.title: UI / UX Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!

We are seeking an experienced UX/UI designer to lead app design projects in Figma for our core application.

We are consistently making changes, improvements, and additions to our nonprofit software.

As our web applications grow, we need to implement strong and dynamic design of our product to ensure our vision is aligned with our developer operations.

As a small growing business, there are a few traits unique to our business that individuals should consider before applying:

 - You must love the idea of giving back to the community and nonprofits. There are thousands of jobs. If being involved in the success of nonprofits is something you’re truly passionate about, this could be a great role.

 - You must be extremely comfortable with change. As a small growing company, decisions can be made over a weekend and fully implemented by early the next week. Whether that’s new systems, new processes, or new approaches, we look for individuals who can adapt and thrive.

 - You must believe that the best contributors should get better rewards. As a company, we believe in paying people based on the value they generate at the business.

 - You must be willing to disagree and commit. As a growing business, there will be lots of decisions that get made and changes that occur during your time at the company. Some of which you’ll agree with and some you won’t, we strongly believe that the best team members have the ability to disagree and commit (i.e., going with a decision after it has been made regardless of whether or not you agree).

 - You must believe the status quo is unacceptable. As a growing company and as individuals, we believe the status quo is unacceptable. Everyone should be relentless in striving to be the best version of themselves and figure out ways to create more value at the company, enhance our client's results, and grow professionally. The question should always be “How can I be better” rather than “How can expectations be adjusted”.




REQUIREMENTS

Location

 - This is a work-from-home setup.



Work Schedule

This role follows a consistent daily rhythm that aligns with US Eastern business hours while giving you time for rest, personal tasks, and balance throughout your day.

Here’s what a typical day looks like:

 - Start your day with a 4.5-hour work block: 9:30am–2:00pm (PHT)

 - Enjoy your afternoon free: 2:00pm–8:00pm (PHT)

 - Finish your workday with another 4.5-hour block: 8:00pm–12:30am (PHT)

 - Sleep/rest: 12:30am–9:30am (PHT)

This structure offers a long midday break while supporting strong collaboration with US-based teams during their business hours.



What Your Typical Day May Look Like

[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/4802dda9-c742-4abd-893c-f4d51c51e50e/37a66765-972b-45cc-9232-80198cf963e2/image.png]

Note: All of these times would shift 1 hour later when the time difference switches to 13 hour differences from 12 hour differences



What Makes a Strong Candidate

 - Based in the Philippines with consistent availability for a long-term role.

 - Highly organized and outstanding organizational ability

 - A team player who has a willingness to learn and grow at a fast-pace

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

We are seeking a highly analytical, strategic, and design-capable Product Designer to drive product development and lead app design projects for Double the Donation’s core application. This role sits at the critical intersection of product strategy and user experience, owning the lifecycle of new features from user research and wireframing to high-fidelity Figma prototyping and engineering handoff.

 

This individual will be responsible for translating complex customer needs and business goals into intuitive, scalable product workflows. We are consistently making changes, improvements, and additions to our nonprofit software. As our web applications grow, you will ensure our product vision is aligned with our developer operations, reducing friction in the user journey and enabling our engineering team to build with clarity and speed.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead end-to-end product feature development, from initial discovery and requirement gathering to final design and implementation

 - Write comprehensive ticket requirements with design documentation and user stories

 - Proficiency in UX/UI design tool Figma for web applications—owning the creation of wireframes, user flows, and high-fidelity prototypes

 - Translate product requirements into multi-module application designs that cleanly manage varying complexities, such as user permissions (admin vs. user roles) and feature tiers (hidden/locked functionality)

 - Proactively identify user pain points, analyze product feedback, and propose effective, data-driven design and feature solutions

 - Collaborate closely with engineers and leadership to ensure proposed tickets and associated designs are technically feasible and meet acceptance criteria
   


SETUP

 - This is a work-from-home setup

 - Target of 42.5 billable hours per week

This role follows a consistent daily rhythm that aligns with US Eastern business hours while giving you time for rest, personal tasks, and balance throughout your day.

 - Finish your workday with another 4.5-hour block: 8:00pm–12:30am (PHT)

This structure offers a long midday break while supporting strong collaboration with US-based teams during their business hours.

Note: All of these times would shift 1 hour later when the time difference switches to 13 hour differences from 12 hour differences



REQUIREMENTS

 - Highly organized and outstanding organizational ability

 - Exceptional English verbal and written communication skills

source_jobs.title: AI-Enabled Copywriter
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

We are seeking a highly productive and detail-oriented AI-Assisted Copywriter to support Double the Donation’s content and SEO initiatives through high-volume, high-quality digital content creation. This role focuses on leveraging AI tools such as ChatGPT to produce conversion-focused copy across web pages, blog articles, landing pages, and digital campaigns while maintaining strong SEO performance, accuracy, and brand alignment.


This is a fast-paced, performance-driven role ideal for individuals who enjoy writing extensively, optimizing workflows with AI, and continuously improving content efficiency without compromising quality.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create high-quality, SEO-optimized content at scale using AI-assisted tools such as ChatGPT and similar platforms

 - Produce conversion-focused copy for blog posts, website pages, landing pages, email campaigns, and other digital marketing assets

 - Consistently meet ambitious weekly and monthly content production goals while maintaining strong quality standards

 - Apply AI tools to accelerate ideation, drafting, editing, and content optimization workflows

 - Ensure all AI-assisted content aligns with SEO best practices, factual accuracy, readability standards, and brand voice guidelines

 - Collaborate closely with the marketing team to develop content targeting high-impact keywords and strategic search opportunities

 - Continuously refine prompts, workflows, and AI-assisted writing processes to improve efficiency and output quality

 - Review, edit, and quality-check AI-generated drafts to ensure publish-ready standards

 - Support ongoing content operations and digital marketing initiatives across the broader team

 - Track content performance insights and contribute ideas for improving engagement, search visibility, and conversion performance
   



SETUP

 - This role follows a consistent daily rhythm that aligns with US Eastern business hours while giving you time for rest, personal tasks, and balance throughout your day.
   
   Here’s what a typical day looks like:
   
   - Start your day with a 4.5-hour work block: 9:30am–2:00pm (PHT)
   
   - Enjoy your afternoon free: 2:00pm–8:00pm (PHT)
   
   - Finish your workday with another 4.5-hour block: 8:00pm–12:30am (PHT)
   
   - Sleep/rest: 12:30am–9:30am (PHT)
   
   This structure offers a long midday break while supporting strong collaboration with US-based teams during their business hours.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

This role focuses on producing engaging videos for webinars, presentations, and social media—helping translate complex fundraising and workplace giving concepts into clear, compelling visual content for nonprofit professionals.

source_jobs.title: Partnership Success and Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!


JOB DESCRIPTION

In this role you’ll be in daily contact with leading nonprofit technology and consulting companies to help build and facilitate partnerships, integrations, and marketing that our nonprofit clients rely on.




THE POSITION:

This is the perfect role for someone who has recently graduated or is early in their career. We’re looking to hire candidates who have 0-3 years experience, an interest in the nonprofit sector, and a desire to build and maintain relationships with a wide variety of people and companies.

As a Partnership Success and Marketing Specialist, your responsibility is to assist in the building and maintenance of the most important relationships our nonprofit clients rely on to keep their technology solutions up and running.

The ideal candidate is excited to represent themselves and Double the Donation to external parties, whether that means presenting a product demo to a team of engineers or coordinating a marketing webinar with an invitee list in the tens of thousands. The best candidates are fantastic written and verbal communicators, quick learners, and skilled researchers.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Spend large portions of your day talking to people in a wide range of positions at nonprofit technology companies

 - Present marketing ideas and strategies that are beneficial for our company, our nonprofit clients, and our partners

 - Coordinate technical, marketing, and commercial conversations and produce supporting materials

 - Prioritize a large amount of possible daily tasks down to a list of essential tasks

 - Represent yourself and the company over the phone, in emails, and sometimes in person

 - Negotiate contracts and close deals
   
   
   


WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - Individuals looking for entry level positions (Majors that could be a good fit include Communications, Business, Public Relations, Journalism, English, Nonprofit Management, Public Administration, or other Liberal Arts degrees)

 - Strong academic performance with an overall 3.9 GPA minimum

 - Individuals who take their professional achievements very seriously
   
   


AS A SMALL GROWING BUSINESS, THERE ARE A FEW TRAITS UNIQUE TO OUR BUSINESS THAT INDIVIDUALS SHOULD CONSIDER BEFORE APPLYING.

 - You must love the idea of giving back to the community and nonprofits. There are thousands of jobs. If being involved in the success of nonprofits is something you’re truly passionate about, this could be a great role.

 - You must be extremely comfortable with change. As a small growing company, decisions can be made over a weekend and fully implemented by early the next week. Whether that’s new systems, new processes, or new approaches, we look for individuals who can adapt and thrive.

source_jobs.title: Community & Events Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!




ROLE

We are seeking a creative and community-oriented Community & Events Specialist to support Double the Donation’s engagement initiatives through educational and community-driven content. This role focuses on producing and supporting digital programming—including webinars, events, and video content—that helps educate nonprofit professionals about matching gifts and workplace giving while strengthening Double the Donation’s community presence.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage ongoing relationships with business partners, helping to support mutually beneficial marketing goals

 - Collaborate with community partners on digital marketing initiatives, helping to drive visibility to our brand

 - Coordinate a wide range of marketing activities with the broader team, supporting lead generation, brand awareness, GEO initiatives, and more

 - Plan, coordinate, and execute virtual events and webinars focused on matching gifts and workplace giving education

 - Create and support the development of educational video content and community-facing resources

 - Develop event and webinar marketing materials, including promotional graphics, presentations, and supporting content

 - Manage event logistics including scheduling, registration, and attendee communications

 - Provide operational and marketing support for digital events and community programming

 - Collaborate with internal teams to align event and video content with product updates, campaigns, and industry insights

 - Repurpose webinar and event content into ongoing educational resources for the community

 - Track engagement and participation metrics to inform future community programming and content strategy
   



SETUP

 - This is a work-from-home setup

 - Target of 42.5 billable hours per week

 - You would work during EST business hours (8 AM EST to 5 PM EST)
   



REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in the Philippines with consistent availability for a long-term role

 - Exceptional English verbal and written communication skills

 - Strong academic background who graduated with an outstanding General Weighted Average

 - Individuals who graduate with a major that could be a good fit including Communications, Business Ad, Public Relations, Journalism, English, Public Administration, or other Liberal Arts and Business degrees

 - Highly organized and outstanding organizational ability

 - Proactive, reliable, and detail-oriented

 - Passionate about marketing, technology, and data-driven results





ORG OVERVIEW

Meet the Team https://vimeo.com/835863867/6c378eac9d

We currently have 100 team members who live in the Philippines who have been fantastic team members and we’re excited to add new team members to this new role!

There are a few traits unique to our business that individuals should consider before applying.

 - You must love the idea of giving back to the community and nonprofits. There are thousands of jobs. If being involved in the success of nonprofits is something you’re truly passionate about, this could be a great role.

 - You must be extremely comfortable with change. As a small growing company, decisions can be made over a weekend and fully implemented

source_jobs.title: Data Insights Specialist (Part-time)
source_jobs.description:
Double the Donation was founded in 2011 and specializes in cloud-based software focused exclusively on the fundraising needs of nonprofit organizations and educational institutions. We've grown to be the leading provider of fundraising software that helps nonprofits and schools increase revenue through corporate matching gift and employee volunteer grant programs.

Thousands of the largest nonprofits in the world like the American Heart Association, Leukemia and Lymphoma Society, Save the Children, Boys and Girls Club of America, as well as hundreds of the top educational institutions like Emory, UGA, Vanderbilt, Case Western, and University of Virginia rely on our services to effectively grow their employee matching gift program revenue.

As a growing company, every employee plays a huge part in our success!



ROLE

We are seeking a Data Insights Specialist to transform raw and cleaned data into actionable insights that drive strategic decisions. This role will support nonprofit and educational outreach, fundraising strategies, and business development initiatives by identifying trends, segmentation opportunities, and high-value targets.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Analyze datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities for outreach and engagement

 - Segment and prioritize nonprofit and educational partners based on potential impact and giving behavior

 - Develop reports, dashboards, and metrics to inform sales, marketing, and program decisions

 - Collaborate with data collection and entity resolution team members to ensure accuracy of insights

 - Continuously refine analytical methods and processes to improve decision-making



SETUP

 - Part-time, 10hrs/week

 - Work-from-home setup



REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of professional experience, ideally within SaaS environments

 - Demonstrated success in previous roles, supported by academic and career achievements

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams



PREFERRED SKILLS

 - Strong SQL and data manipulation skills (e.g., Python/Pandas, Excel Power Query, or similar tools)

 - Familiarity with matching techniques such as fuzzy matching, probabilistic linkage, and rule-based logic

 - Experience working with CRMs or data warehouses

 - Background in data quality, normalization, and data governance best practices
   
   

ORG OVERVIEW

With 90+ Filipino team members already thriving in our company, we are excited to expand this growing team with new talent! You'll be working with our product, data, tech teams that are split between the US & Philippines.

There are a few traits unique to our business that individuals should consider before applying.

 - You must love the idea of giving back to the community and nonprofits. There are thousands of jobs. If being involved in the success of nonprofits is something you’re truly passionate about, this could be a great role.

 - You must be extremely comfortable with change. As a small growing company, decisions can be made over a weekend and fully implemented by early the next week. Whether that’s new systems, new processes, or new approaches, we look for individuals who can adapt and thrive.

 - You must believe that the best contributors should get better rewards. As a company, we believe in paying people based on the value they generate at the business.

 - You must be willing to disagree and commit. As a growing business, there will be lots of decisions that get made and changes that occur during your time at the company. Some of which you’ll agree with and some you won’t, we strongly believe that the best team members have the ability to disagree and commit (i.e., going with a decision after it has been made regardless of whether or not you agree).

 - You must believe the status quo is unacceptable. As a growing company and as individuals, we believe the status quo is unacceptable. Everyone should be relentless in striving to be the best version of thems

notes: Software company with a strong focus on fundraising and nonprofit organizations.
